page: Microsoft Azure D48d_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It includes 1843 GB of local storage, while lacking any dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results place the D48d_v5 in the middle tier for general compute tasks, with a single-core stress-ng score of 1899.29 and a multi-core score of 46630.34. However, it demonstrates strong performance in specific workloads, ranking in the top 25% for Passmark database operations and Passmark CPU encryption tests. Image processing via Geekbench also registers in the top quartile. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 2499.33 GB/sec, which drops to 152.26 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48d_v5 offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise workloads requiring a mix of compute, memory, and high-capacity local storage. The inclusion of 1843 GB of local storage provides a cost-effective alternative to attaching external block storage for temporary data or caching. However, the lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for heavy machine learning training, though it maintains respectable CPU-based LLM text generation speeds for small models. This instance is well-suited for relational databases, web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure D48d_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the DDv5 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It is configured with a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor offering 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and a 2.8 GHz clock speed. The hardware profile includes 192.0 GB of RAM and 1843 GB of local storage, with no GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate solid mid-range capabilities, with strong performance in database operations, CPU encryption, and image processing. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and large local storage capacity make this instance highly suitable for database hosting, web serving, and software compilation. It offers a balanced resource density, eliminating the need for external storage for high-speed local caching.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48d_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage, delivering strong performance for database operations and encryption.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48d_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage, optimized for databases and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring high-capacity local storage and strong database performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
192 GB of system memory and 1843 GB of local storage
Strong benchmark performance in database operations and encryption
Balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio for general-purpose workloads
Ideal for database hosting, web serving, and software compilation
